message:
feat(err): add warnc + vwarnc + errc + verrc (bd-vcma4)

BSD/NetBSD <err.h> explicit-code variants. Where warn/err read the
strerror suffix from the global errno, the *c family takes an
explicit `code` parameter — useful when the caller has already
preserved errno or wants to report a different code than the one
currently set.

* warnc(code, fmt, ...) / vwarnc(code, fmt, ap)
* errc(eval, code, fmt, ...) / verrc(eval, code, fmt, ap) — exit(eval)

Implementation factors a new write_err_message_with_code helper that
reuses the existing get_progname / printf-render / strerror /
format_err_message pipeline; the only difference vs. write_err_message
is the strerror lookup uses the supplied code rather than the global.
Global errno is preserved across the call (matches warn/err contract).

12 integration tests for warnc/vwarnc covering: code differs from
global, NULL fmt, code=0 success, format args, rapid fire, NULL
va_list, errno preservation, concurrent calls. errc/verrc are
exit-on-call so they cannot be tested in-process (same constraint
as the existing err/errx tests). Clippy-clean, fmt-clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
